name: unstructured-ingest

metadata.name: unstructured-ingest
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: A library that prepares raw documents for downstream ML tasks.

documentation:
# Unstructured Ingest  

For details, see the [Unstructured Ingest overview](https://docs.unstructured.io/ingestion/overview) in the Unstructured documentation.

functions.name: test.unit.test_interfaces.test_partition_file
documentation:
Validate partition_file returns a list of dictionaries with the expected keys,
metadatakeys, and data source metadata values.

functions.name: test.unit.test_interfaces.test_process_file_fields_include_default
documentation:
Validate when metadata_include and metadata_exclude are not set, all fields:
("element_id", "text", "type", "metadata") are included

functions.name: test.unit.test_interfaces.test_process_file_metadata_includes_filename_and_filetype
documentation:
Validate when metadata_include is set to "filename,filetype",
only filename is included in metadata

functions.name: test.unit.test_interfaces.test_process_file_metadata_exclude_filename_pagenum
documentation:
Validate when metadata_exclude is set to "filename,page_number",
neither filename nor page_number are included in metadata

classes.name: test.unit.test_interfaces.DescribeChunkingConfig
documentation: Unit tests for unstructured.ingest.interfaces.ChunkingConfig

functions.name: test.integration.embedders.utils.validate_embedding_output
documentation:
Make sure the following characteristics are met:
* The same number of elements are returned
* For each element that had text, an embeddings entry was added in the output
* Other than the embedding, nothing about the element was changed

functions.name: test.integration.connectors.test_onedrive.test_onedrive_destination
documentation:
Integration test for the OneDrive destination connector.

This test uploads a file to OneDrive and verifies that it exists.

classes.name: unstructured_ingest.pipeline.interfaces.PipelineContext
documentation: Data that gets shared across each pipeline node

classes.name: unstructured_ingest.pipeline.interfaces.PipelineNode
documentation: Class that encapsulates logic to run during a single pipeline step

classes.name: unstructured_ingest.pipeline.interfaces.DocFactoryNode
documentation: Encapsulated logic to generate a list of ingest docs

classes.name: unstructured_ingest.pipeline.interfaces.SourceNode
documentation:
A pipeline node representing logic to pull data from a source using base ingest documents.

This class encapsulates the logic for pulling data from a specified source using base ingest
documents. The output of this logic is expected to be in JSON format representing the data
itself.

Attributes:
    read_config: A configuration object specifying how to read data from the source.
    retry_strategy_config: Optional configuration specifying the strategy for network errors.

Properties:
    retry_strategy: A retry handler configured based on the retry strategy configuration.

Methods:
    initialize: Initializes the source node and logs the process.
    run: Abstract method for downloading data associated with ingest documents.

classes.name: unstructured_ingest.pipeline.interfaces.PartitionNode
documentation: Encapsulates logic to run partition on the json files as the output of the source node

classes.name: unstructured_ingest.pipeline.interfaces.ReformatNode
documentation:
Encapsulated any logic to reformat the output List[Element]
content from partition before writing it

classes.name: unstructured_ingest.pipeline.interfaces.WriteNode
documentation: Encapsulated logic to write the final result to a downstream data connection

classes.name: unstructured_ingest.pipeline.interfaces.CopyNode
documentation: Encapsulated logic to copy the final result of the pipeline to the designated output location.

classes.name: unstructured_ingest.pipeline.interfaces.PermissionsNode
documentation: Encapsulated logic to do operations on permissions related data.

classes.name: unstructured_ingest.enhanced_dataclass.json_mixin.EnhancedDataClassJsonMixin
documentation:
A mixin class extending DataClassJsonMixin.

This class extends the functionality of DataClassJsonMixin to provide enhanced functionality
for JSON serialization and deserialization. It introduces options for redacting sensitive
information, custom encoding, and more advanced schema handling.

Attributes:
    N/A (No additional attributes)

Methods:
    to_json: Serialize the object to JSON format with customizable options.
    from_dict: Deserialize a dictionary into an object of this class.
    to_dict: Convert the object to a dictionary with customizable options.
    schema: Generate a schema for validating and parsing JSON data based on this class.

functions.name: unstructured_ingest.enhanced_dataclass.core._asdict
documentation:
A re-implementation of `asdict` (based on the original in the `dataclasses`
source) to support arbitrary Collection and Mapping types.

functions.name: unstructured_ingest.logger.hide_sensitive_fields
documentation:
Will recursively look through every k, v pair in this dict and any nested ones and run
is_sensitive_fn to dynamically redact the value of the k, v pair. Will also check if
any string value can be parsed as valid json and process that dict as well and replace
the original string with the json.dumps() version of the redacted dict.

functions.name: unstructured_ingest.logger.redact_jsons
documentation:
Takes in a generic string and pulls out all valid json content. Leverages
hide_sensitive_fields() to redact any sensitive information and replaces the
original json with the new redacted format. There can be any number of valid
jsons in a generic string and this will work. Having extra '{' without a
closing '}' will cause this to break though. i.e '{ text, {"a": 3}'.

functions.name: unstructured_ingest.logger.make_default_logger
documentation: Return a custom logger.

Class encapsulating fetching a message and writing processed results.

classes.name: unstructured_ingest.connector.kafka.KafkaSourceConnector
documentation:
Source connector for Kafka.
Main job is to consume from a Kafka topic and create instances of
KakfaIngestDoc.
Note that messages have the format of:
<filename>: the name of the file (with correct file extension)
<content>: base64 encoded (whether was binary or not)

classes.name: unstructured_ingest.connector.kafka.KafkaDestinationConnector
documentation:
Connector to write BaseIngestDoc types to Kafka
Writes messages to Kafka in the format:
"type"<type>
"text":<the partitioned text>
"filename":<name of the upstream file>

modules.name: unstructured_ingest.connector.salesforce
documentation:
Salesforce Connector
Able to download Account, Case, Campaign, EmailMessage, Lead
Salesforce returns everything as a list of json.
This saves each entry as a separate file to be partitioned.
Using JWT authorization
https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.sfdx_dev.meta/sfdx_dev/sfdx_dev_auth_key_and_cert.htm
https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.sfdx_dev.meta/sfdx_dev/sfdx_dev_auth_connected_app.htm

functions.name: unstructured_ingest.connector.jira.nested_object_to_field_getter

limitless templates to parse an api response object.

functions.name: unstructured_ingest.connector.jira.form_templated_string

Forms a template string via parsing the fields from the API response object on the issue
The template string will be saved to the disk, and then will be processed by partition.

api_token is the api token to authenticate into Atlassian (Jira) Cloud,
url is the URL pointing to the Atlassian (Jira) Cloud instance,
list_of_projects is a list of project that is aimed to be ingested.

Check ...
for more info on the api_token.

functions.name: unstructured_ingest.connector.google_drive.create_service_account_object
documentation:
Creates a service object for interacting with Google Drive.

Providing a drive id enforces a key validation process.

Args:
    key_path: Path to Google Drive service account json file. (or the actual json)
    id: ID of a file on Google Drive. File has to be either publicly accessible or accessible
        to the service account.

Returns:
    Service account object

modules.name: unstructured_ingest.connector.fsspec.dropbox
documentation:
Dropbox Connector
The Dropbox Connector presents a couple abnormal situations.
1) They don't have an unexpiring token
2) They require a forward slash `/` in front of the remote_file_path. This presents
some real problems creating paths. When appending a path that begins with a
forward slash to any path, whether using the / shorthand or joinpath, causes the
starting path to disappear. So the `/` needs to be stripped off.
3) To list and get files from the root directory Dropbox you need a ""," ", or " /"

classes.name: unstructured_ingest.connector.fsspec.dropbox.MissingFolderError
documentation: There is no folder by that name. For root try `dropbox:// /`

modules.name: unstructured_ingest.connector.fsspec.box
documentation:
Box Connector
Box does not make it simple to download files with an App.
First of all, this does not work with a free Box account.
Make sure the App service email is a collaborator for your folder (co-owner or editor)
Make sure you have the 'write all files' application scope
Maybe check 'Make api calls as the as-user header'
REAUTHORIZE app after making any of the above changes

Provides a wrapper for a function that catches any exception and
re-raises it as the customer error. If the exception itself is already an instance
of the custom error, re-raises original error.

functions.name: unstructured_ingest.cli.utils.extract_config
documentation:
To be able to extract a nested dataclass from a flat dictionary (as in one coming
from a click-based options input), the config class is dynamically looked through for
nested dataclass fields and new nested dictionaries are created to conform to the
shape the overall class expects when parsing from a dict. During the process, this will create
copies of the original dictionary to avoid pruning fields but this isn't a
problem since the `from_dict()` method ignores unneeded values.

Not handling more complex edge cases for now such as nested types i.e Union[List[List[...]]]

functions.name: unstructured_ingest.cli.utils.extract_configs
documentation:
Extract all common configs used across CLI command and validate that any
command-specific configs have all their needed information from the Click
options that are passed in during invocation.

methods.name: unstructured_ingest.cli.utils.Group.parse_args
documentation:
This allows for subcommands to be called with the --help flag without breaking
if parent command is missing any of its required parameters

methods.name: unstructured_ingest.cli.utils.Group.format_commands

Copy of the original click.Group format_commands() method but replacing

functions.name: unstructured_ingest.cli.cli.get_cmd
documentation:
Construct and return a Click command object representing the main command for the CLI.

This function adds all dest_subcommand(s) to each src_subcommand, and adds all of those
to the main command as nested subcommands.

functions.name: unstructured_ingest.utils.chunking.id_to_hash
documentation:
Calculates and assigns a deterministic hash as an ID.

The hash ID is based on element's text, sequence number on page,
page number and its filename.

Args:
    sequence_number: index on page

Returns: new ID value

functions.name: unstructured_ingest.utils.dep_check.requires_dependencies
documentation:
Decorator ensuring required modules are installed.

Use on functions with local imports to ensure required modules are available and log
an installation instruction if they're not.

Args:
    dependencies: Name(s) of module(s) required by the decorated function.
    extras: unstructured-ingest extra which installs required `dependencies`. Defaults to None.

Raises:
    ImportError: When at least one of the `dependencies` is not available.

documentation: A helper function to break an iterable into batches of size batch_size.

functions.name: unstructured_ingest.utils.data_prep.flatten_dict
documentation:
Flattens a nested dictionary into a single level dictionary.

keys_to_omit is a list of keys that don't get flattened. If omitting a nested key, format as
{parent_key}{separator}{key}. If flatten_lists is True, then lists and tuples are flattened as
well. If remove_none is True, then None keys/values are removed from the flattened
dictionary.

functions.name: unstructured_ingest.utils.data_prep.validate_date_args
documentation:
Validate whether the provided date string satisfies any of the supported date formats.

Used by unstructured/ingest/connector/biomed.py

Returns `True` if the date string satisfies any of the supported formats, otherwise raises
`ValueError`.

Supported Date Formats:
    - 'YYYY-MM-DD'
    - 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS'
    - 'YYYY-MM-DD+HH:MM:SS'
    - 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS±HHMM'

Extension of the dataclass from_dict() to avoid a naming conflict with other CLI params.
This allows CLI arguments to be prepended with embedding_ during CLI invocation but
doesn't require that as part of the field names in this class

documentation:
Extension of dataclass from_dict() to avoid a naming conflict with other CLI params.

This allows CLI arguments to be prefixed with "chunking_" during CLI invocation but doesn't
require that as part of the field names in this class

methods.name: unstructured_ingest.cli.interfaces.CliPermissionsConfig.from_dict
documentation:
Extension of the dataclass from_dict() to avoid a naming conflict with other CLI params.
This allows CLI arguments to be prepended with permissions_ during CLI invocation but
doesn't require that as part of the field names in this class. It also checks if the
CLI params are provided as intended.

modules.name: unstructured_ingest.v2.processes.connectors.salesforce
documentation:
Salesforce Connector
Able to download Account, Case, Campaign, EmailMessage, Lead
Salesforce returns everything as a list of json.
This saves each entry as a separate file to be partitioned.
Using JWT authorization
https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.sfdx_dev.meta/sfdx_dev/sfdx_dev_auth_key_and_cert.htm
https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.sfdx_dev.meta/sfdx_dev/sfdx_dev_auth_connected_app.htm

through Unstructured.

variables.name: unstructured_ingest.interfaces.InitVar.__call__
documentation: type: ignore

classes.name: unstructured_ingest.interfaces.BaseSessionHandle
documentation:
Abstract Base Class for sharing resources that are local to an individual process.
e.g., a connection for making a request for fetching documents.

classes.name: unstructured_ingest.interfaces.AccessConfig
documentation:
Meant to designate holding any sensitive information associated with other configs
and also for access specific configs.

classes.name: unstructured_ingest.interfaces.RetryStrategyConfig
documentation:
Contains all info needed for decorator to pull from `self` for backoff
and retry triggered by exception.

Args:
    max_retries: The maximum number of attempts to make before giving
        up. Once exhausted, the exception will be allowed to escape.
        The default value of None means there is no limit to the
        number of tries. If a callable is passed, it will be
        evaluated at runtime and its return value used.
    max_retry_time: The maximum total amount of time to try for before
        giving up. Once expired, the exception will be allowed to
        escape. If a callable is passed, it will be
        evaluated at runtime and its return value used.

methods.name: unstructured_ingest.interfaces.ChunkingConfig.__post_init__
documentation:
Resolve chunking_strategy if chunk_elements is True.

If chunk_elements is True and chunking_strategy is None, default to 'by_title'. Otherwise,
do nothing and keep the defined value of chunking_strategy."

Decorator that checks if a file exists, is not empty, and should not re-download,

methods.name: unstructured_ingest.interfaces.BaseSingleIngestDoc.update_source_metadata
documentation: Sets the SourceMetadata and the  properties for the doc
TODO: set as @abstractmethod and pass or raise NotImplementedError

methods.name: unstructured_ingest.interfaces.BaseSingleIngestDoc.update_permissions_data
documentation:
Sets the _permissions_data property for the doc.
This property is later used to fill the corresponding SourceMetadata.permissions_data field,
and after that carries on to the permissions_data property.

methods.name: unstructured_ingest.interfaces.BaseSingleIngestDoc.get_file
documentation: Fetches the "remote" doc and stores it locally on the filesystem.
NOTE(crag): Future BaseIngestDoc classes could define get_file_object() methods
in addition to or instead of get_file()

methods.name: unstructured_ingest.interfaces.BaseSingleIngestDoc.has_output
documentation: Determine if structured output for this doc already exists.

An "ingest document" is specific to a connector, and provides
methods to fetch a single raw document, store it locally for processing, any cleanup
needed after successful processing of the doc, and the ability to write the doc's
structured outputs once processed.

Crucially, it is not responsible for the actual processing of the raw document.

methods.name: unstructured_ingest.interfaces.BaseIngestDocBatch.get_files
documentation: Fetches the "remote" docs and stores it locally on the filesystem.

classes.name: unstructured_ingest.interfaces.BaseSourceConnector

methods.name: unstructured_ingest.interfaces.BaseSourceConnector.cleanup
documentation:
Any additional cleanup up need after processing is complete. E.g., removing
temporary download dirs that are empty.

By convention, documents that failed to process are typically not cleaned up.

methods.name: unstructured_ingest.interfaces.BaseSourceConnector.initialize
documentation:
Initializes the connector. Should also validate the connector is properly
configured: e.g., list a single a document from the source.

methods.name: unstructured_ingest.interfaces.BaseSourceConnector.get_ingest_docs
documentation:
Returns all ingest docs (derived from BaseIngestDoc).
This does not imply downloading all the raw documents themselves,
rather each IngestDoc is capable of fetching its content (in another process)
with IngestDoc.get_file().
Abstract Base Class for a connector to a remote source, e.g. S3 or Google Drive.

methods.name: unstructured_ingest.v2.processes.connectors.gitlab.GitLabConnectionConfig.set_repo_path
documentation:
Parses the provided GitLab URL to extract the `base_url` and `repo_path`,
ensuring both are properly formatted for use.

If the URL contains a scheme (e.g., 'https') and a network location (e.g., 'gitlab.com'),
the `base_url` is set accordingly. The repository path is extracted and normalized
by removing any leading slashes.

Notes:
    - If the URL contains both a scheme and network location, the `base_url` is
      extracted directly from the URL.
    - The `repo_path` is adjusted to remove any leading slashes.
    - This method assumes that the URL follows GitLab's structure
      (e.g., 'https://gitlab.com/owner/repo').

methods.name: unstructured_ingest.v2.processes.connectors.gitlab.GitLabConnectionConfig.get_project
documentation:
Retrieves the specified GitLab project using the configured base URL and access token.

Returns:
    Project: A GitLab `Project` object representing the specified repository.

Raises:
    SourceConnectionError: If the GitLab API connection fails.
    gitlab.exceptions.GitlabGetError: If the project is not found.

methods.name: unstructured_ingest.v2.processes.connectors.gitlab.GitLabIndexer.precheck
documentation:
Validates the connection to the GitLab instance by authenticating or
accessing the project.

This method ensures that the GitLab credentials and configuration are correct by
either authenticating or attempting to fetch the specified project.

Raises:
    SourceConnectionError: If the connection or authentication with GitLab fails.

methods.name: unstructured_ingest.v2.processes.connectors.gitlab.GitLabIndexer.run
documentation:
Iterates over the GitLab repository tree and yields file metadata as `FileData` objects.

This method fetches the repository tree for the specified branch and iterates
over its contents. For each file (blob), it generates a `FileData` object containing
the file's metadata, path, and permissions.

Args:
    **kwargs (Any): Additional keyword arguments (if required).

Yields:
    FileData: A generator that yields `FileData` objects representing each file (blob)
    in the repository.

methods.name: unstructured_ingest.v2.processes.connectors.gitlab.GitLabDownloader.run
documentation:
Downloads a file from the repository and returns a `DownloadResponse`.

Args:
    file_data (FileData): Metadata about the file to be downloaded.
    **kwargs (Any): Additional arguments (if required).

Returns:
    DownloadResponse: A response object containing the download details.

class_variables.name: unstructured_ingest.v2.processes.connectors.milvus.MilvusUploadStagerConfig.fields_to_include

If set - list of fields to include in the output.
Unspecified fields are removed from the elements.
This action takes place after metadata flattening.
Missing fields will cause stager to throw KeyError.

and also for access specific configs.

modules.name: unstructured_ingest.v2.constants
documentation: Used to append to metadata for uploaders that store element-level data

functions.name: unstructured_ingest.v2.unstructured_api.create_partition_request
documentation:
Given a filename and a dict of API parameters, return a PartitionRequest for use
by unstructured-client. Remove any params that aren't recognized by the SDK.

Args:
    filename: Path to the file being partitioned
    parameters_dict: A mapping of all API params we want to send

Returns: A PartitionRequest containing the file and all valid params

functions.name: unstructured_ingest.v2.unstructured_api.call_api_async
documentation:
Call the Unstructured API using unstructured-client.

Args:
    server_url: The base URL where the API is hosted
    api_key: The user's API key (can be empty if this is a self hosted API)
    filename: Path to the file being partitioned
    api_parameters: A dict containing the requested API parameters

Returns: A list of the file's elements, or an empty list if there was an error

functions.name: unstructured_ingest.v2.unstructured_api.call_api
documentation:
Call the Unstructured API using unstructured-client.

Args:
    server_url: The base URL where the API is hosted
    api_key: The user's API key (can be empty if this is a self hosted API)
    filename: Path to the file being partitioned
    api_parameters: A dict containing the requested API parameters

Returns: A list of the file's elements, or an empty list if there was an error

functions.name: unstructured_ingest.v2.logger.hide_sensitive_fields
documentation:
Will recursively look through every k, v pair in this dict and any nested ones and run
is_sensitive_fn to dynamically redact the value of the k, v pair. Will also check if
any string value can be parsed as valid json and process that dict as well and replace
the original string with the json.dumps() version of the redacted dict.

functions.name: unstructured_ingest.v2.logger.redact_jsons
documentation:
Takes in a generic string and pulls out all valid json content. Leverages
hide_sensitive_fields() to redact any sensitive information and replaces the
original json with the new redacted format. There can be any number of valid
jsons in a generic string and this will work. Having extra '{' without a
closing '}' will cause this to break though. i.e '{ text, {"a": 3}'.

functions.name: unstructured_ingest.v2.logger.make_default_logger
documentation: Return a custom logger.

functions.name: unstructured_ingest.v2.cli.cli.get_cmd
documentation:
Construct and return a Click command object representing the main command for the CLI.

This function adds all dest_subcommand(s) to each src_subcommand, and adds all of those
to the main command as nested subcommands.

classes.name: unstructured_ingest.v2.cli.utils.model_conversion._RangeDict
documentation: Represent arguments to `click.IntRange` or `click.FloatRange`.
